events.title: WORKSHOP: Pitch Principles
events.description: Pitch Principles is an interactive, experiential workshop designed to help students transform the way they present their ideas. Too often, pitches become rehearsed scripts delivered to slides instead of compelling stories delivered to people. In this session, students will learn how to communicate with confidence, clarity, and authentic presence.

events.title: Creative Leadership Forum
events.description: Join Lions & Rabbits Center for the Arts March 28th at Bamboo Grand Rapids for our Creative Leadership Forum!

events.title: Powering Communities: Building Partnerships for Electric Transportation
events.description: This workshop explores how Midwest communities are advancing clean, affordable electric mobility through strategic partnerships, effective community engagement, and equitable planning. HOSTED BY: Forth and Toward Equitable Electric Mobility (TEEM)

events.title: The Quarter Evenings
events.description: ​A weekly peer group for founders building from $0 to $250k, focused on customers and revenue, not fundraising.

events.title: speaker series: Jim Mallekoote
events.description: Jim Mallekoote spent nearly 30 years at Apple, serving as Senior Director of Retail Sales and working directly with Steve Jobs in both Apple’s early and revival eras. He played a key role in launching the original iPod team in 2002, helping Apple transition from a computer company to a consumer technology leader. Jim will share behind-the-scenes stories and leadership lessons from one of the most innovative companies in the world.

events.title: AI for Manufacturing: Hands On Training Series
events.description: This free 4-session course helps companies benefit from AI without the cost or risk of a full enterprise-wide rollout.

events.title: Members Lunch Break
events.description: ​Lunch Break is our weekly community lunch. Every Thursday at noon, all Bamboo GR members are invited to gather in the 5th floor kitchen for lunch together.

events.title: First Friday Update
events.time: April 3 @ 10:00 am - 10:30 am First Friday Update

events.title: That's It, I've Had Enough, I'm Starting My Own Company
events.description: Have you ever thought of starting your own company? Ensure you are going out on your own with all the tools and support possible. We will discuss many of the decisions you need to make when creating a startup, like what type of insurance is needed, should contracts be given to all clients, and I have too much work, what do I do? HOSTED BY: Lansing Regional SmartZone

events.title: PitchMI Championship
events.description: Four teams are advancing to the statewide championship, where they’ll go head-to-head at 517 E&I Week in Greater Lansing for an additional $1 million investment and the title of PitchMI Champion. HOSTED BY: MSU Research Foundation and Michigan Economic Development Corporation (MEDC)

events.title: Innovation Celebration
events.description: This annual event recognizes the vision and impact of MSU faculty, students, researchers, and inventors whose work is shaping solutions to real-world challenges and contributing to Michigan’s economic growth. ​Guests are invited to tour the exhibition space to explore new technologies developed across campus. The program also honors the 2026 awardees in innovation, entrepreneurship, technology transfer, and sponsored research.

events.title: The Quarter Evenings
events.description: ​A weekly peer group for founders building from $0 to $250k, focused on customers and revenue, not fundraising.

events.title: April Happy Hour (ABA)
events.description: Our April Happy Hour on April 8th from 4:30-6:30pm, will be hosted at Gravel Bottom Craft Brewery. Enjoy a relaxed evening of networking, conversation, and community connection in one of Ada’s favorite gathering spots. Whether you’re reconnecting with fellow members or meeting new faces, this casual after-hours event is a great way to stay engaged with the ABA community. We look forward to seeing you on April 8th at Gravel Bottom Craft Brewery.

events.title: Bringing Your Online Business to Life with AI
events.description: Accepting Payments with Stripe: This session focuses on what makes modern digital businesses possible: accepting online payments. After the standard intro to AI development and hosting workflows, we’ll walk through using Stripe’s low-code tools to sell a product, subscription, or event registration. HOSTED BY: Lansing Regional SmartZone

events.title: Innovate State: From Supply Chain to Global Change
events.description: The Innovate State Speaker Series brings emerging and accomplished Spartan entrepreneurs—innovators, builders, creators, and visionaries—to campus for a live, unscripted fireside chat. These 60-minute sessions offer the chance for guests to share their perspectives on a wide array of topics like innovation, startup life, investments, and career paths. HOSTED BY: MSU Burgess Institute for Entrepreneurship & Innovation

events.title: Workshop: Pitch Like A Pro
events.description: The Pitch Like a Pro Workshop offers an engaging, hands‑on environment for anyone looking to build confidence in their pitch - whether they’re new to pitching or simply want extra practice. Participants receive practical guidance and supportive feedback to help them communicate their ideas clearly and compellingly. While the workshop is open to all aspiring pitchers, there is a special invitation for students competing in MWest the following week, giving them a valuable opportunity to refine their pitch, strengthen their delivery, and gain added confidence before stepping into the competition.

events.title: Student Venture Capital Fund Investment Celebration 2026
events.description: This event showcases MSU FI 444 Entrepreneurial Finance students as they present Michigan-based startups recommended for pre-seed investment. HOSTED BY: The MSU Research Foundation and the Broad College of Business Center of Venture Capital, Private Equity, and Entrepreneurial Finance

events.title: 2026 Burgess New Venture Challenge
events.description: Burgess New Venture Challenge is the premier pitch competition for student entrepreneurs at Michigan State University. On Thursday, April 9, 2025, as part of 517 Entrepreneurship & Innovation Week , fifteen student semifinalists will participate in a transformational experience to compete for $50,000+ in funding. HOSTED BY: Burgess Institute for Entrepreneurship & Innovation

events.title: Business Made Simple: The Stunio Story with Adam Green
events.description: ​In this session, Adam Green, President & CEO of Stunio, will share the story behind building a platform that connects employers with students for one-day shifts, internships, and part-time work. HOSTED BY: LEAP, Lansing Regional SmartZone

events.title: Distilling Innovation
events.description: Join us during Northern Michigan Startup Week at Mammoth Distilling for a panel and candid conversation about what it takes to turn ideas into companies. Founders, investors, and researchers will share their experiences building ventures rooted in Michigan innovation—from space technology to sustainable craft beverage systems and heritage agriculture.

events.title: Midwest Marketing Meetup
events.description: Whether you're seasoned or new to the industry, connect with West Michigan's marketing community and hear real insights from someone in the trenches.

events.title: Workshop: Build an app in two hours
events.description: In this hands-on workshop, Jon VerLee will guide early-stage founders and aspiring entrepreneurs through the process of turning an app idea into a functional MVP. Using modern tools and practical frameworks, you’ll learn how to move from concept to creation quickly - no technical experience necessary. By the end, you’ll walk away with a working MVP you can begin testing, validating, and refining with real users.

events.title: OutPro Forum: The Business of Belonging
speaker series: Sheena Brown
events.description: Sheena Brown is the founder of Fatirah’s Designs and a creative visionary with over 20 years of experience designing and producing transformational events. A former special education educator turned entrepreneur, Sheena blends artistic excellence with deep care for client experience. Recognized in national and local publications, she will share her journey of building a creative business rooted in vision, purpose, and excellence.
